items.definition_en: A blackcurrant is a small dark berry with a strong flavor.
items.example_en: She added blackcurrant jam to the yogurt.

items.definition_en: A fig is a sweet fruit with soft flesh and many tiny seeds.
items.example_en: He picked a ripe fig from the tree and ate it.

items.definition_en: Passion fruit is a tropical fruit with a tough rind and seed-filled interior.
items.example_en: I love the tangy taste of passion fruit in smoothies.

items.definition_en: A persimmon is an orange fruit that is sweet when ripe.
items.example_en: I ate a persimmon for the first time last week.

items.definition_en: A kumquat is a small citrus fruit that you can eat whole.
items.example_en: She brought a bowl of kumquats for us to try.

items.definition_en: A lychee is a small round fruit with sweet white flesh.
items.example_en: Lychee is one of my favorite fruits to eat in summer.

items.definition_en: A rambutan is a tropical fruit with hairy skin and juicy flesh.
items.example_en: The market had fresh rambutan for sale today.

items.definition_en: A quince is a hard yellow fruit often used in cooking.
items.example_en: She made quince jelly from the fruit she picked.

items.definition_en: A gooseberry is a small green or red berry with a tart taste.
items.example_en: He grew gooseberries in his garden every year.

items.definition_en: A mandarin is a type of seedless, easy-peeling orange.
items.example_en: I gave my friend a mandarin for a snack.

items.definition_en: A cranberry is a small, red, tart berry often used in sauces.
items.example_en: Cranberry juice is especially popular during the holidays.

items.definition_en: A mulberry is a sweet purple or white fruit that grows on trees.
items.example_en: We collected mulberries from the tree in the backyard.
